goal: Add mainline doctor for local orphan drafts

summary.title: Add mainline doctor for orphan drafts
summary.what: Introduced a new mainline doctor command and engine service method that inspect local draft cache health. The first doctor check reports drafting intents whose local git branch no longer exists as orphan drafts, reports stale drafting intents separately, and deletes only orphan draft files when --fix is provided.
summary.why: Dogfood found old local drafting intents left from early workflows. They do not pollute remote team state, but they clutter local log/status surfaces and can be mistaken for active work. A doctor command gives mainline a place for local repair checks without making normal commands destructive.
summary.user_goal: Create an extensible mainline doctor command that can clean local orphan drafts now and host more repair checks later.

decisions.chose: Report-only by default, destructive cleanup only behind --fix
decisions.rationale: Local draft files may contain useful recovery context. Reporting first avoids deleting user work unexpectedly.

decisions.chose: A drafting intent is orphaned when its recorded local git branch is missing and it is not the current branch
decisions.rationale: This is a conservative signal that the draft is no longer reachable through the normal branch-based workflow.

decisions.chose: Report stale drafting intents separately and never delete them automatically
decisions.rationale: A stale draft on an existing branch may still be intentional or recoverable work.

decisions.chose: Add engine.Doctor with DoctorOptions and DoctorResult rather than baking repair logic only into CLI
decisions.rationale: Future doctor checks can share structured JSON output and tests while the CLI remains a thin renderer.

rejected.reason: Age alone is not enough proof that a draft is abandoned.

rejected.alternative: Clean local drafts during sync or status
rejected.reason: Those commands should not unexpectedly mutate local recovery state.

risks: A user may still need to manually abandon or delete stale drafts on existing branches because doctor only auto-fixes missing-branch orphans.
The branch-missing heuristic is intentionally conservative and may leave some real stale drafts for future doctor checks.
followups: Add doctor checks for local proposed drafts that are already merged in the materialized view.
Add a repair path for stale current-branch drafts that asks the user to abandon or keep them.
Consider branch/worktree health checks under the same doctor command.

architectural_claims: Doctor repair logic lives in engine.Service and returns structured results for CLI and JSON consumers.
The initial doctor repair scope is local-only draft cache maintenance; it does not mutate actor logs or remote state.
Destructive doctor behavior is opt-in via DoctorOptions.Fix and the CLI --fix flag.
behavioral_changes: mainline doctor reports orphan local drafting intents whose recorded branch no longer exists.
mainline doctor --fix deletes orphan draft and turn files through Store.DeleteDraft.
mainline doctor reports stale drafting intents based on --stale-after without deleting them.
mainline doctor supports JSON output through the existing global --json path.
security_implications: mainline doctor --fix deletes local cache files under .ml-cache/drafts for detected orphan drafts; it does not touch tracked source files or remote refs.
